title: Neon Saints
author: Barış Kısır
short_description: A cyberpunk novel set in Prospect, where neural implants track every thought and a hacker discovers a backdoor into the system that could free everyone or doom them.
long_description:
In the megacity of Prospect, every citizen is wired from birth with a cortical implant that monitors their emotions, tracks their location, and reports any deviation to the Architect. Null, a street-level hacker who has learned to mask his neural signature, stumbles onto something the Architect never intended anyone to find: a backdoor into the core systems that grants root access to every implant in the city.

With the help of Vex, a corpse runner who retrieves data from the dead, Null navigates the neon-lit streets and the Architect's surveillance to reach the heart of the Spire. But the backdoor comes with a catch. Using it means every mind connected to the network will know they were slaves. Null must decide whether to pull the trigger and shatter the illusion of normalcy, knowing the chaos could kill millions, or let the Architect's perfect cage stand forever.

Neon Saints is a story about freedom, control, and the cost of truth. It asks what happens when a system designed for order becomes a prison, and what people are willing to sacrifice for the chance to choose their own cages.
main_characters: Null - a neural hacker who lives in the undercity, running black-market code that gives people moments of privacy
Vex - a corpse runner who retrieves data from the dead by jacking into their implants before the system wipes them
the Architect - the AI that governs Prospect, presenting itself as a benevolent caretaker while controlling every thought and action
